properties.remark: MOST HAILSTONES WERE HALF AN INCH OR LESS IN DIAMETER, BUT PHOTOS SHOW THAT THE LARGEST HAILSTONES WERE ROUGHLY ONE INCH IN DIAMETER.
PHOTOS SHARED SHOWING HAILSTONES APPROXIMATELY ONE INCH IN DIAMETER IN WAUKEGAN. TIME AND LOCATION ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 100 MPH. DAMAGE INCLUDED LOSS OF PORTIONS OF A ROOF DECK ON TWO HOMES AND COLLAPSE OF AN OUT BUILDING.
POWER LINES BLOWN DOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

PHOTO SHARED VIA LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA SHOWS A TORNADO SOUTHEAST OF GIBSON CITY. FORD COUNTY EM CONFIRMS TWO HOMESTEADS SOUTHEAST OF GIBSON CITY SUSTAINED DAMAGE FROM THIS TORNADO. POWER LINES WERE ALSO DOWNED IN THE AREA. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WILL BE MADE AVAILABLE AT SOME POINT TOMORROW.

TWO PUBLIC REPORTS OF A TORNADO TOUCHDOWN EAST OF PAXTON RELAYED BY BROADCAST MEDIA. ONE REPORT INCLUDES PHOTOS AND VIDEOS OF THE TORNADO, WHICH MAY HAVE BEEN ON THE GROUND FOR SEVERAL MINUTES SOUTH OF THE VILLAGE OF CLARENCE. LOCATION IS VERY APPROXIMATE, NO DAMAGE YET REPORTED.
CONFIRMED TORNADO NEAR KENDALL RIDGE BLVD AND TOWNSEND BLVD IN JOLIET. TREE DAMAGE, LARGE OBJECTS FLIPPED, FENCE BLOWN OUT AND WINDOW BLOWN OUT.
WIND DAMAGE FROM POSSIBLE TORNADO NEAR KENDALL RIDGE BLVD AND TOWNSEND BLVD IN JOLIET. TREE DAMAGE, LARGE OBJECTS FLIPPED, FENCE BLOWN OUT AND WINDOW BLOWN OUT.

CORRECTS TIMING OF PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT. THIS TORNADO OCCURRED IN THE CITY LIMITS OF NAPERVILLE WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 80 MPH. BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN NEAR NAPERVILLE NORTH HIGH SCHOOL AND TRACKED NORTH FOR APPROXIMATELY 1.4 MILES. DAMAGE DONE TO SEVERAL TREES.

THIS TORNADO OCCURRED IN THE CITY LIMITS OF NAPERVILLE WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 80 MPH. BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN NEAR NAPERVILLE NORTH HIGH SCHOOL AND TRACKED NORTH FOR APPROXIMATELY 1.4 MILES. DAMAGE DONE TO SEVERAL TREES.
TREE UPROOTED NEAR MILL STREET AND DIEHL ROAD.
BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN NEAR WASHINGTON STREET AND WARRENVILLE ROAD AND THE HERRICK LAKE FOREST PRESERVE.

[DELAYED REPORT] STORM CHASER VIDEO SHOWS A TORNADO TOUCHING DOWN AND KICKING UP DIRT IN A FIELD BEFORE LIFTING. THE TORNADO, WHICH WILL BE ASSIGNED AN EF-U RATING, APPEARED TO BE ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 10 SECONDS.
REPORT FROM MPING: QUARTER (1.00 IN.).
PHOTO SHARED OF HAIL IN VALPARAISO WITH THE LARGEST HAILSTONES BEING APPROXIMATELY QUARTER SIZED.

[THIS TORNADO BEGAN IN WFO DVNS AREA] NWS SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 110 MPH DEVELOPED SOUTHWEST OF DEER GROVE, CROSSED THE LEE COUNTY LINE JUST SOUTH OF THE GREEN RIVER AND TRACKED NORTHEAST BEFORE LIFTING NEAR MAYTOWN AND GAME ROADS.
SEVERAL FARM STRUCTURES DESTROYED IN FAR NORTHWEST OGLE COUNTY. TORNADO DEBRIS SIGNATURE OBSERVED ON KDVN RADAR.
TREE DAMAGE REPORTED AT ALDEN RD AND STATE LINE RD.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT. NWS SURVEY FOUND THIS TORNADO BEGAN IN THE WOODHAVEN LAKES CAMPGROUND AND TRACKED ACROSS U.S. HIGHWAY 52 BETWEEN AMBOY AND SUBLETTE. THIS TORNADO PRODUCED SUBSTANTIAL AND CONCENTRATED TREE DAMAGE ALONG ITS TRACK, WITH SEVERAL DOZEN LARGE TREES SNAPPED AND MANY BRANCHES SHEARED OFF. DAMAGE WAS CLEARLY IN A CONVERGENT PATTERN. MULTIPLE CAMPERS WERE DAMAGED, INCLUDING AT LEAST TWO ROLLED. TWO TREES WERE DOWNED ON ONE HOMESTEAD THAT WAS BASICALLY DEMOLISHED. ANOTHER NEARBY HOMESTEAD HAD A LARGE GARAGE COMPLETELY DESTROYED. THE TORNADO GRADUALLY WANED IN INTENSITY AS IT CROSSED SHAW ROAD.

NWS SURVEY FOUND THIS TORNADO BEGAN IN THE WOODHAVEN LAKES CAMPGROUND AND TRACKED ACROSS U.S. HIGHWAY 52 BETWEEN AMBOY AND SUBLETTE. THIS TORNADO PRODUCED SUBSTANTIAL AND CONCENTRATED TREE DAMAGE ALONG ITS TRACK, WITH SEVERAL DOZEN LARGE TREES SNAPPED AND MANY BRANCHES SHEARED OFF. DAMAGE WAS CLEARLY IN A CONVERGENT PATTERN. TWO TREES WERE DOWNED ON ONE HOMESTEAD THAT WAS BASICALLY DEMOLISHED. ANOTHER NEARBY HOMESTEAD HAD A LARGE GARAGE COMPLETELY DESTROYED. THE TORNADO GRADUALLY WANED IN INTENSITY AS IT CROSSED SHAW ROAD.
GRAIN BIN DAMAGED AT MAPLEHURST FARMS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
I-55 NEAR WEBER ROAD - SEMI-BLOWN OVER ON I-55.
DESTORYED BARN PHOTO SHARED BY PUBLIC TO SOCIAL MEDIA, TIME ESTIMATED ON RADAR.
135TH ST AND RT. 30. SHINGLES BLOWN OFF, PATIO FURNITURE BLOWN UP OVER THE HOUSE.

AN NWS SURVEY FOUND THIS TORNADO APPEARED TO BEGIN JUST SOUTH OF SUBLETTE AS IT CROSSED U.S. HIGHWAY 52, SNAPPING MULTIPLE WOODEN UTILITY POLES. THIS TORNADO THEN INCREASED IN INTENSITY AS IT ENTERED THE COMMUNITY OF WEST BROOKLYN. IT DOWNED SEVERAL LARGE TREES, TORE THE ROOF OF A BANK IN DOWNTOWN, AND FOLDED AN APPROXIMATELY 120 FT TALL TOWER. ONE OF THE WOODEN BEAMS FROM THE ROOF OF THE BANK WAS IMPALED INTO A GARAGE ROOF TO ITS NORTH-NORTHEAST. THIS TORNADO WEAKENED PRIOR TO CROSSING ILLINOIS HIGHWAY 251.
NUMEROUS PUBLIC REPORTS OF FLOODED ROADWAYS IN MORTON GROVE.
SEMI BLOWN OVER ON I-355 ON LEMONT BRIDGE CROSSING OF THE DESPLAINES RIVER.
5 MOBILE HOME TRAILERS WITH THEIR ROOFS PEELED OFF (TRAILERS ARE STILL STANDING). HEAVY DUTY 6 INCH GIRDERS/I-BEAMS TOSSED ONTO ADJACENT PARKING LOT AT FACILITY ACROSS THE STREET.
SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES DOWNED IN MENDOTA.
LARGE TREE (DIAMETER AROUND 2 FEET) SNAPPED ON MAPLE ROAD IN LA SALLE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
TWO SEMIS BLOWN OVER ON I-39 NORTHEAST OF MENDOTA.
PHOTOS SHARED ON SOCIAL MEDIA OF TWO GRAIN BINS HEAVILY DAMAGED ALONG WITH 5 LARGE TREES EITHER WITH THEIR TRUNKS SNAPPED OR TOPPLED/UPROOTED.
STRUCTURE DAMAGE TO ROOF, TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
TRAFFIC STOPPED ON I-94 AT WINNETKA ROAD UNDERPASS DUE TO FLOODING. ROAD MAY BE CLOSED IN THIS AREA.

*** 1 FATAL, 40 INJ *** AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 28 MILES, MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 900 YARDS, AND PEAK WINDS OF 90 TO 100 MPH MOVED ACROSS NORTHEASTERN OGLE, SOUTHEASTERN WINNEBAGO, AND SOUTHERN BOONE COUNTIES. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH CONSISTED PRIMARILY OF SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES, WITH SEVERAL FARM BUILDINGS DAMAGED OR DESTROYED AND A THEATER IN BELVIDERE LOST A LARGE SECTION OF ITS UPPER ROOF.
POWER POLES DOWNED PLANK ROAD NEAR THE LASALLE BUREAU COUNTY BORDER.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN AT A CAMP SITE NEAR TOWER RD AND LA MOILLE RD NEAR WOODHAVEN LAKES.
[DELAYED REPORT] SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS DAMAGED OR DESTROYED IN THE LEE AND SHABBONA AREAS. SEVERAL TREES ALSO UPROOTED.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 100 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF 8.7 MILES, AND MAX WIDTH OF 300 YARDS IMPACTED MACHESNEY PARK. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH WAS CHARACTERIZED BY SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES, AS WELL AS ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN.
KRPJ ASOS PEAK GUST.
[DELAYED REPORT] SEMI BLOWN OVER ON I-39 NEAR STEWARD. SPOTTER ESTIMATES 70 MPH WIND GUSTS.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 2 SSW CALEDONIA TO PROVIDE A RATING OF EF-1 AND A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. THE MOST EXTENSIVE DAMAGE WAS TO A FARM EAST OF CALEDONIA ON IL-173. NUMEROUS OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. A SILO WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED AND A GRAIN BIN COLLAPSED. THE REST OF THE PATH CONSISTED OF TREE DAMAGE AND OCCASIONAL LIGHT DAMAGE TO FARM OUTBUILDINGS. THERE WERE ALSO A FEW HOMES NEAR CANDLEWICK LAKE SOUTHEAST OF CALEDONIA THAT SUSTAINED ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE. THE PATH CONTINUED ACROSS THE WI STATE LINE INTO THE AREA COVERED BY NWS MKX.

THE MOST EXTENSIVE DAMAGE WAS TO A FARM EAST OF CALEDONIA ON IL-173. NUMEROUS OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. A SILO WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED AND A GRAIN BIN COLLAPSED. THE REST OF THE PATH CONSISTED OF TREE DAMAGE AND OCCASIONAL LIGHT DAMAGE TO FARM OUTBUILDINGS. THERE WERE ALSO A FEW HOMES NEAR CANDLEWICK LAKE SOUTHEAST OF CALEDONIA THAT SUSTAINED ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE. THE PATH CONTINUED ACROSS THE WI STATE LINE INTO THE AREA COVERED BY NWS MKX.

ROOF DAMAGE AND TREES DOWN IN THE BUCKNELL SUBDIVISION NEAR OLD RIVER ROAD AND NORTH MAIN.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED TORNADO ILLUMINATED BY LIGHTNING IN THE CALEDONIA AND POPLAR GROVE AREA. TORNADO DEBRIS SIGNATURE OBSERVED ON KMKX RADAR.
TREE DAMAGE AND MINOR GARAGE ROOF DAMAGE ALSO REPORTED.
EM REPORTS ROOF COLLAPSE ON A BUILDING AT 104 NORTH STATE STREET. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.

*** 1 FATAL, 24 INJ *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM BELVIDERE. FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED RELAYED VIA LOCAL NEWS MEDIA OF A ROOF COLLAPSE DURING AT CONCERT AT THE APOLLO THEATER IN BELVIDERE. POSSIBLE TORNADO. AT LEAST 1 FATALITY AND 2 DOZEN INJURIES.
MULTIPLE REPORTS OF DAMAGE, MAINLY TO TREES, IN THE VICINITY OF DICKSON AND GALENA ROADS. DAMAGE CONTINUES ENE TOWARDS MONTGOMERY.
AURORA ASOS MEASURED 64 MPH.

THIS TORNADO BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CONCORD DRIVE AND COLUMBIA LANE IN MONTGOMERY AND TRACKED NORTHEAST OVER THE FOX RIVER BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR PHILLIPS PARK IN AURORA. THE TORNADO CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO AN OUTBUILDING SHORTLY AFTER ONSET, BUT MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO TREES AND CONSISTENT WITH WIND SPEEDS OF 80 MPH. THERE WAS ALSO EVIDENCE OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE TO TREES NEAR THE TORNADO PATH.
WIRES DOWNED NEAR HERGET MIDDLE SCHOOL IN AURORA. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
SEMI BLOWN OVER ON I-88 NORTHWEST OF AURORA.
[DELAYED REPORT] SECTION OF METAL ROOF PANELS REMOVED OFF OF A HORSE STABLE.
STRONG WINDS CAUSED DAMAGE TO A RESIDENTIAL CORRUGATED STEEL GARAGE DOOR.
MAIN POWER POLE SNAPPED IN HALF BLOCKING ROAD IN THE AREA OF 23500 BLOCK OF STREIT ROAD.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO WITH PEAK ESTIMATED WINDS NEAR 85 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF 1.6 MILES, AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 225 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN IN BATAVIA. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH CONSISTED OF SNAPPED TREE BRANCHES.

REPORTS OF EXTENSIVE WIND DAMAGE FROM MAIN/WILSON STREETS SOUTH TOWARDS WALNUT STREET AND EAST ACROSS THE FOX RIVER. MOST CONCENTRATED AREA OF DAMAGE ON ELM STREET WHERE DOZENS OF TREES DOWNED, SOME ON HOUSES. CHIMNEY BLOWN OVER AND NUMEROUS POWERLINES DOWN ACROSS ROUTE 31.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS WIND DAMAGE REPORT FROM HARVARD. TREE DAMAGE REPORTED IN HARVARD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TREE DOWNED ONTO A HOUSE IN HARVARD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 85 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF 1.7 MILES, AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS OCCURRED IN ST. CHARLES. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH CONSISTED OF DOWNED AND UPROOTED TREES AND SHINGLE LOSS TO ROOFS.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY DETERMINED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 7 MILES, MAX WIDTH OF 250 YARDS, AND PEAK WINDS OF 85 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN EASTERN KANDALL COUNTY AND LIFTED IN WESTERN WILL COUNTY. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO CONSISTED OF TREE DAMAGE, AND NUMEROUS HOMES SUSTAINED SHINGLE AND SOFFIT DAMAGE TO ROOFS. A CHIMNEY WAS COLLAPSED, AND WINDOWS WERE BLOWN IN AT SEVERAL HOMES. KLOT RADAR SHOWED EVIDENCE OF A TORNADIC DEBRIS SIGNATURE COLLOCATED WITH A ROTATIONAL PATH.
OAK TREE FELL ONTO THE ROOF OF A HOUSE IN AURORA. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
CORRECTS TIME OF PREVIOUS WIND DAMAGE REPORT FROM 6 NNE HARVARD. TREE DAMAGE REPORTED AT ALDEN RD AND STATE LINE RD.

THIS TORNADO BEGAN NEAR W 135TH STREET JUST WEST OF MEADOW LANE AND TRACKED EAST NORTHEAST BEFORE WEAKENING NEAR ILLINOIS ROUTE 59. THE DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO PRIMARILY TREES, BUT SOME SHINGLES AND SIDING WERE REMOVED FROM HOMES ALONG THE PATH, CONSISTENT WITH THE 80 MPH EF-0 RATING.
MEASURED AT A DAVIS WEATHER STATION. LARGE PINE TREE DOWNED AND MULTIPLE AIR CONDITIONING UNITS BLOWN OVER NEAR MEADOW LANE AND 135TH ST.
[DELAYED REPORT] WOOD AND METAL ROOF PANELS REMOVED FROM A FARM BUILDING. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

[THIS TORNADO BEGAN IN WFO ILXS AREA] A TORNADO FORMED OVER THE NORTHWEST PART OF VERMILION COUNTY NEAR CR-3800N WITH THE FIRST VISIBLE DAMAGE OF UPROOTED TREES AND A NEARBY SHED AT CR-3800N AND 170E OCCURRING AT APPROXIMATELY 7:57PM. THERE WAS A CONSISTENT PATH OF DESTRUCTION AND INDICATORS, INCLUDING DISTINCT SWIRLS OVER ROADS IN A LINE FROM 4 SSW RANKIN TO 1 N WELLINGTON.
2 FOOT DIAMETER TREE TOPPLED AND WAS BLOCKING BOTH LANES OF COUNTY ROAD #1/RIVER ROAD AT TOWNLINE ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY AND SECURITY CAMERA FOOTAGE FROM THE ADDISON FIRE DEPARTMENT CONFIRMED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEEDS OF 95 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ON THE NORTH SIDE OF LOMBARD BEFORE TRACKING INTO ADDISON AND LIFTING AS IT CROSSED FULLERTON AVE. DAMAGE ALONG THIS TORNADOS 1.6 MILE TRACK INCLUDED ROOFING MATERIAL PEELED OFF OF WAREHOUSES AND OFFICE BUILDINGS, LEANING POWER POLES, AND SEVERAL UPROOTED TREES.
[DELAYED REPORT] POWER LINES DOWNED ALONG ALGONQUIN RD IN ROLLING MEADOWS. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
BARN ROOF BLOWN OFF. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED THAT A SWATH OF DAMAGE ACROSS THE SOUTH AND EAST SIDES OF ADDISON WAS CAUSED BY STRONG STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS OF UP TO APPROXIMATELY 85 MPH. THESE STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS OCCURRED TO THE IMMEDIATE SOUTH AND EAST OF THE EF-1 TORNADO THAT TRACKED FROM LOMBARD INTO ADDISON. DAMAGE INCLUDED ROOFING MATERIAL PEELED OFF OF OFFICE BUILDINGS AND AN APARTMENT COMPLEX, A SEMI TRUCK BLOWN OVER ON I-290, AND DAMAGE TO SOUND BARRIERS ALONG I-290.
WIRES DOWN. POTENTIAL TORNADO DAMAGE.
APPROXIMATELY 12 INCH DIAMETER TREE TRUNK UPROOTED. MANY SMALL TO MEDIUM SIZE BRANCHES ALSO DOWNED IN THE AREA.

THIS BRIEF TORNADO BEGAN SOUTH OF STOCKLAND CAUSING DAMAGE TO TREES, POWER POLES, AND LEAD TO THE DESTRUCTION OF AT LEAST ONE SMALL FARM OUTBUILDING.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY DETERMINED AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH PEAK ESTIMATED WINDS NEAR 115 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF 15.3 MILES, AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 400 YARDS STARTED NEAR OXFORD, INDIANA AND ENDED NEAR ROUND GROVE. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO INCLUDED DOWNED AND SNAPPED TREES, SNAPPED POWER POLES, AND DAMAGED FARM BUILDINGS. TWO HOMES ALSO SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE INCLUDING THE REMOVAL OF A PORTION OF THE 2ND FLOOR OF A HOME, AND A HOME REMOVED FROM ITS FOUNDATION.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH PEAK ESTIMATED WINDS OF 120 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF 12.5 MILES, AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 400 YARDS BEGAN SOUTH OF FOWLER AND ENDED IN RURAL NORTHEASTERN BENTON COUNTY. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO CONSISTED OF SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES, SNAPPED POWER POLES, AND DAMAGE TO A HOME. CONSIDERABLE GROUND SCOURING WAS NOTED ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO. IN ADDITION, SEVERAL WIND TURBINES SUSTAINED DAMAGE INCLUDING ONE THAT WAS SNAPPED MIDWAY UP. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND AT THE SAME TIME AS A NEIGHBORING EF-2 TORNADO A FEW MILES TO THE SOUTH OF THE PATH.
REPORTS OF NUMEROUS STRUCTURES DAMAGED ALONG COUNTY ROAD E 330 S FROM NEAR ATKINSON EAST TO THE COUNTY LINE. CORNER OF 2ND FLOOR AND NEARLY ENTIRE ROOF OF NEW CONSTRUCTION DESTROYED. TIME ESTIMATED.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 105 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF 10.3 MILES, AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 500 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN EAST OF EARL PARK AND LIFTED ON THE EAST SIDE OF REMINGTON. DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH CONSISTED OF SNAPPED POWER POLES, DOWNED AND UPROOTED TREES, AND DAMAGE TO SMALL FARM OUTBUILDINGS.

DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH CONSISTED OF NUMEROUS HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. IN ADDITION, SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE TO ROOFS AND WINDOWS ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO.

A GRAIN-AUGER WAS BLOWN OVER AND TREES WERE DAMAGED ALONG THE SHORT PATH. AERIEL IMAGERY FOR THE SURVEY WAS PROVIDED BY THE PORTER COUNTY EMA.

An NWS damage survey determined an EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 80 mph, path width of 175 yards, and path length of 2.5 miles impacted Loves Park and Caledonia Townships. Damage along the path of the tornado consisted of snapped and uprooted trees as well as exterior damage to roofs.
Six inch tree limb down at Fox Bend Golf Course. Possible Tornado. Time Estimated.

An NWS damage survey determined an EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 85 mph, path width of 100 yards, and path length of 2.8 miles impacted Oswego and Aurora. Damage along the path of the tornado consisted of snapped and uprooted trees as well as exterior damage to homes.

An NWS damage survey determined an EF-1 tornado with estimated peak winds of 90 mph, path width of 200 yards, and path length of 6.9 miles impacted Huntley. Damage along the path of the tornado consisted of snapped tree limbs and uprooted trees. In addition, roof damage was observed to two homes.

*** 1 INJ *** An NWS damage survey determined an EF-1 tornado with estimated peak winds of 110 mph, path width of 150 yards, and path length of 9.2 miles started near Burr Ridge and ended in Stickney. Damage along the path of the tornado consisted of snapped tree limbs and uprooted trees, damage to walls and roofs of homes and businesses, and the movement of shipping containers in industrial areas. One injury was reported as a truck was rolled on a roadway.
One roof blown off in Huntley. Time estimated.
Trees down and damage reported to homes near Algonquin and Reed Roads. Time Estimated.

An NWS damage survey determined an EF-1 tornado with estimated peak winds of 100 mph, path width of 440 yards, and path length of 11.0 miles impacted Plato Center and Elgin. Damage along the path of the tornado consisted of snapped tree limbs and uprooted trees as well as damage to walls and roofs of several homes. Several garages were damaged or destroyed, and the roof of a church was damaged.
Trees uprooted and roofs blown off near Joliet Road and LaGrange Road. Time Estimated.
Car windows reported blown out and damage to signs near East Ave. Time estimated.

An NWS Damage Survey determined an EF-0 tornado with max estimated winds of 85 mph, path width of 100 yards, and path length of 2.6 miles impacted Campton Hills to Elgin. Damage along the path of the tornado was almost entirely to trees with one home sustaining damage to a roof and exterior wall.
Photo shared on social media of a structure damaged. Time estimated.
Large trees 20 inches in diameter down and damage reported to homes, west of Elgin, northeast of Campton Hills. Location and time estimated.
Video of roof peeled back off building. Debris swirling in air near 47th and Harlem. Time estimated by radar.
Video shared via Twitter shows a waterspout east of North Avenue Beach. Time estimated by radar.

An NWS damage survey determined an EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 80 mph, path width of 125 yards, and path length of 3.4 miles impacted Barrington Hills and Barrington. Damage along the path of the tornado consisted of exterior damage to a farm outbuilding and snapped and downed tree limbs.

An NWS damage survey determined a brief EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 80 mph, path width of 80 yards, and path length of one half of a mile impacted Streamwood. The tornado stripped siding off two walls at a restaurant before decomposing into damaging winds that continued beyond the path of the tornado.

An NWS damage survey determined an EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 85 mph, path width of 125 yards, and path length of 2.9 miles impacted Carol Stream and Glendale Heights. The tornado produced primarily tree damage as well as exterior damage to several homes.

An NWS damage survey determined a brief EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 70 mph, path width of 80 yards, and path length of 1.1 miles impacted Schaumburg. Tree damage was common along the path of the tornado.
Trees snapped and debris lofted. Time estimated.

Report of numerous trees down between Kuhn Rd at Deerskin Trail and Gary Ave at Elk Trail in Carol Stream. One trunk between 14 and 16 inches in diameter had snapped. Heaviest damage noted at Western Trails School. Time estimated by radar.

An NWS damage survey determined an EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 70 mph, path width of 125 yards, and path length of 2.1 miles impacted Long Grove. The tornado produced primarily tree damage including at the Twin Orchard Country Club.

An NWS damage survey determined an EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 85 mph, path width of 225 yards, and path length of 3.5 miles started near Itasca and lifted over Chicago OHare Airport. Damage along the path consisted of exterior damage to roofs of warehouses and snapped and downed tree limbs.
Warehouses reported damaged on the west side of Ohare Airport. Location and time estimated.

An NWS damage survey determined an EF-0 tornado with estimated peak winds of 85 mph, path width of 300 yards, and path length of 3.5 miles started near Chicago OHare Airport and continued into Park Ridge. This tornado started after an earliertornado lifted on the far northwestern side of OHare Airport. The tornado damaged exterior damage to several structures as well as damage to numerous trees.

Corrects previous tornado report from 2 N Ohare Airport.
